To make matters worse, Dotty mentions the pills found in Bex's room, leaving Louise shocked. Meanwhile, the Slaters remind Kush that Walford is no safe place for Martin, Sharon is desperate to meet Mel's demands and Jean is not prepared for what Daniel says to her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef

Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael. programme.title: Fleabag

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day seven of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Katarina Johnson-Thompson's bid to usurp Nafi Thiam as heptathlon champion concludes with the javelin and 800m. Thiam is the reigning world and Olympic title-holder, but Johnson-Thompson is the world indoor pentahlon champion and will hope to finally win a senior outdoor gold on the world stage. The men's multi-eventers are also on show as the decathlon draws to a close with the pole vault, javelin and 1500m, with France's reigning champion and world record-holder Kevin Mayer expected to be in contention. Elsewhere, medals are decided in the women's shot put and 400m, with China's Gong Lijiao and America's Phyllis Francis hoping to be in the mix to defend the titles they won in 2017. With analysis from Jessica Ennis-Hill and Denise Lewis(n)

programme.desc: The two remaining designers compete in the final of the competition, and are challenged to redesign an entire open plan apartment in Manchester. Before the finalists start the design process, knowing they need to make it count, they head off to Manchester, to see the apartments for the first time. Meeting the homeowners, they realise they must design for the client, but also work hard to put their own design stamp on the properties. With just three days to complete this ambitious challenge, the finalists know they have their work cut out if they're to prove to Michelle that they are the worthy winner(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sharon Osbourne investigates her family history. Recalling her less-than-idyllic childhood and her remote relationship with her mother, Sharon delves into her maternal family history and uncovers the difficult circumstances of her mother's and grandmother's lives. Later, Sharon discovers that she had a great grandmother who was born in the United States, but soon comes to realise that her great-great-grandparent's hope of living the American Dream did not go according to plan when they were faced with the harsh reality of work in a cotton mill(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Northamptonshire to create pieces for people they have never met. Taking part are an abstract artist who paints in a nuclear tent, an illustrator who wants to make his most ambitious painting to date, and a bronze-sculptor who is pushed for time(n)

Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost gives advice on how to prune overgrown evergreen shrubs and adds to his borders to create height. Rachel de Thame shows how to maintain containers and refresh with plants that will extend the display into autumn, and there's a feature on a gardener whose frequent absences from home have influenced the design and planting of his garden(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Colourful milkshake funnel cakes have all the fun of fresh doughnuts with none of the fuss. A giant time-saving cookie is so easy it's fried in the pan, and a glistening nutty baklava looks so impressive but is completely fool-proof. On the road, Nadiya joins the team Tate and Lyle on the River Thames, to find out how they produce one of her favourite fast-tracks to flavour - golden syrup(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House proud couples from Northamptonshire and Surrey swap homes and give their verdicts on the lifestyles of complete strangers. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: The Sky's the Limit (1943)
programme.desc: A Second World War hero returns home to New York and sneaks into town incognito to avoid a big fuss. He meets the girl of his dreams, but his efforts to court her are thwarted when she leaps to the conclusion that he is a coward trying to get out of going to war. Romantic musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire and Joan Leslie, with an appearance by bandleader Freddie Slack(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson uncover a few gems including a silver ladle with a surprise in its bowl and a train set brought in by the son of William Moorcroft, the world-famous ceramicist. Plus, Paul finds out about the epic 18th-century voyage by the second Englishman to circumnavigate the globe(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of day eight of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Home favourite Mutaz Essa Barshim is his nation's only defending world champion, and will be looking to take high jump gold once again this evening, despite recent injury issues. Likewise, South Africa's Wayde van Niekerk will be hoping to put his own injury troubles behind him to prevail once again in the 400m. Medals are also decided in the women's 400m hurdles, in which 20-year-old American Sydney McLaughlin was the pre-event favourite, and finals take place in the women's discus and men's 3,000m steeplechase. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Mary Berry comes to tea with Monty Don and, as well has giving her a tour of Longmeadow, he offers his advice on her new garden. Frances Tophill meets gardeners on an allotment in Birmingham and harvests some unusual vegetables, while Adam Frost and Arit Anderson join forces to transform a front garden in Hampshire into a haven for the owners as well as wildlife. In Nottinghamshire, the programme meets an enthusiast who has amassed a collection of over 100 varieties of rhubarb and in Scotland a herb grower with over 600 different types(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into the topic of Questions and Qualifications, with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Ade Adepitan, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, with Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: A programme from Glasgow, focusing on the cultural influence of food, exploring what people eat can say about who they are as individuals, communities and nations? Meanwhile, presenter Mary Beard is join by Great British Bake Off judge Prue Leith to take a tour of an exhibition of art and artefacts at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, which explores food culture in ancient Pompeii(n)

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: The lives of composer Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, whose romance began on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of foreign artists, writers, and musicians. This film follows their relationship, from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, to how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician. Along the way the film tells of the tragedy that befell those that could not survive the beauty of Hydra, the highs and lows of Leonard's career, and the inspirational power that Marianne possessed(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: The presenter continues in his quest to understand everyday objects by putting them back together again, this time reassembling the 147 pieces of an electric guitar. James' task will entail soldering, extensive use of his precision Japanese screwdrivers, and some fiddly electronics, before he attempts to channel Brian May in a solo performance(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian travels to Lough Foyle to explore the history of a Flying Fortress that crashed while travelling from America to an air force base near Bedford. He unearths the stories of the crew's survival against the odds and learns how a local woman and her boat contributed to their rescue. He also visits the disused Cluntoe airfield in Co Tyrone, where RAF pilots were trained for flying missions all over Europe, and looks back at the climatic end to the Battle of the Atlantic on the River Foyle docks(n)

programme.title: A Day in the Life of Earth
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry examines how much the planet can change in just 24 hours and how these daily changes are essential to humanity's existence. She looks at how new land is created by lava from volcanoes, and that the Moon not only causes huge movements of water in the oceans in the form of tides, but also creates waves of solid rock on land(n)

programme.title: Rik Mayall: Lord of Misrule
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Rik Mayall died suddenly in June 2014, but left a body of work that spanned four decades and was a blend of rocket-fuelled physical comedy, surrealism, subversive satire and pompous punk wit. Mayall's characters included the Black Country investigative nerd Kevin Turvey, Felicity Kendal-adoring student poet Rick in The Young Ones, unemployed oddball Richard Richard in Bottom, ruthless MP Alan B'Stard in The New Statesman and the roguish Lord Flashheart, who appeared in the second and fourth series of Blackadder. Narrated by Simon Callow, this documentary celebrates Mayall's part in British comedy history, with contributions by Michael Palin, Simon Pegg, Lenny Henry, Ben Elton, Alexei Sayle, Christopher Ryan, Tim McInnerny, Jools Holland, Ruby Wax and Greg Davies(n)

programme.title: Stories from the Dark Earth: Meet the Ancestors Revisited
programme.desc: Archaeologist Julian Richards revisits some of his most important digs to discover how science, conservation and new finds have changed people's understanding of ancient history. His journey continues with two excavations that illustrate the vastly different social roles occupied by a pair of Iron Age women. The first site uncovers evidence of the possible ritual sacrifice of a teenage girl from the Cotswolds, while the second unearths the chariot burial of a queen from 400BC, whose well preserved possessions are leading to astonishing conclusions about belief in early times(n)

programme.title: A Fanatic Heart: Geldof on Yeats
programme.desc: The musician and campaigner examines the life and work of poet WB Yeats, arguing that he was a key figure in a cultural revolution and that his writing played a major role in Ireland's struggle for independence. Featuring readings of Yeats' verse by Bill Nighy, Van Morrison, Colin Farrell, Bono, Edna O'Brien, Ardal O'Hanlon, Noel Gallagher and Liam Neeson(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Violence flares up in the wake of an incident in which a police officer shot an unarmed man, and a sense of injustice causes Josephine vent her frustration in the Bodin case. Meanwhile, Tintin feels like he is being intentionally kept in the dark. French crime drama, starring Audrey Fleurot(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Laure and her team turn their attention to tracking down missing teen Maria, when it becomes clear their work in gathering evidence against Drissa Camara and the corrupt cops is going nowhere fast. Meanwhile, Roban in unnerved when he is forced to testify in court in his own defence - cross-examined by Josephine. French crime drama, starring Caroline Proust(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Andy Crane present the edition that first aired on July 21, featuring music by Kim Wilde, Everything But the Girl and S-Express(n)

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece(n)

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson, reveal that eugenics - the controversial idea that was a driving force behind the Nazi death camps - originated in the upper echelons of the British scientific community. They also examine its legacy and continued influence today(n)

programme.title: The Science of D-Day
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell examines the science and innovations that made the D-Day landings of June 6, 1944 possible, from tanks that could drive on water to gigantic gliders that carried thousands of troops to the beaches of Normandy. However, like all new inventions not all of them were successful - with devastating consequences(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents reaction to day seven of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, including a look at the final events of the heptathlon and decathlon. With analysis Jessica Ennis-Hill and Denise Lewis(n)

programme.title: Bute: The Scot Who Spent a Welsh Fortune
programme.desc: Suzanne Packer narrates the story of Victorian aristocrat John Patrick Crichton-Stuart, the third Marquess of Bute, who financed the construction of distinctive Welsh landmarks including the gothic Cardiff Castle, and Castell Coch. One of the richest men in his era, Lord Bute had a passion for architecture, but is also reported to have been a forward-thinking intellectual who supported women's right and striking miners, and Suzanne explores how Wales became the beneficiary of this visionary Scottish lord's extravagant tastes(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Andy Crane present the edition that first aired on July 21, featuring music by Kim Wilde, Everything But the Girl and S-Express(n)

programme.title: B Is for Book
programme.desc: Following a group of primary school children over the course of a year as they learn to read, with some making a flying start as others struggle to learn the alphabet. The film also reveals how much help they receive at home from their parents, some of whom only speak English as a foreign language(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Drissa Camara is betrayed, but hopes his hold over Gilou will save him, and as the secrets and lies begin to overwhelm Tintin, Josephine realises stickling to her story will place her in very real jeopardy. French crime drama, starring Audrey Fleurot(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: With their numbers dangerously depleted, the detective team makes a catastrophic error that could have a shocking outcome. programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition first aired on July 28, with performers including Pat Benatar, Shakin' Stevens and Glenn Medeiros(n)

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri(n)

programme.title: Betty Davis: Godmother of Funk
programme.desc: Profile of the aspiring songwriter, who arrived on the '70s scene with her daring personality, iconic fashion and outrageous funk music. She befriended Jimi Hendrix and Sly Stone, wrote songs for the Chambers Brothers and the Commodores, and married Miles Davis - turning him from jazz to funk on the album she named Bitches Brew. Betty was a feminist pioneer, inspiring and intimidating in a manner like no woman before. This documentary charts her rise from humble upbringings to take the genre by storm, revealing the mystery of her subsequent disappearance and her battle with mental illness(n)

programme.title: The Story of Funk: One Nation Under a Groove
programme.desc: Documentary examining the history of funk, starting from its birth in the black community at a time of self-discovery, struggle and social change. The fun style of the music changed the streets of the US in the seventies with new trends in fashion and slang emerging, and its celebration of African-American culture. The genre produced some of the most famous acts in the world, such as James Brown, Sly & the Family Stone, Earth, Wind & Fire, and Kool & the Gang. Including interviews with George Clinton, Ray Parker Jr and trombonist Fred Wesley(n)

programme.desc: Aimee and Ashley are joined by a Dixie the Dog, dressed as crafty spider, and Bobbles the Bear as the sweet and trusting fly for Mary Botham Howitt's tale The Spider and the Fly(n)

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day explains about the challenges faced by the newborns of animals such as wildebeest, penguins and caiman, and how they rely on their parents to get them around(n)

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Mr Bogglelots, the optician, visits the school to give the little monsters a very special eye test. Wufflebump gets the test wrong on purpose just to get a pair of googly but soon realises that wearing the wrong glasses isn't good for your eyes(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Sisters Gracie and Myla and their dad use papier mache to make a pinata filled with goodies, and the girls also dress up as astronauts(n)

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African folk tale explaining why flamingos stand on only one leg(n)

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Laia is cooking a delicious lentil stew from Guinea-Bissau called Kansiye(n)

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Shelly's pink recipe becomes a blobby mess when it is hit by Salty's ball, reminding Jack of the time they encountered a similar looking fish(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie worries about Grannie Island, who having hurt her back and unable to get out of bed, does not want to trouble anyone. The youngster decides to ask Granma Mainland for help(n)

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: The girl and her mother visit a farm, and Melody listens to music that makes her think of a fox and chicken dancing(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Aleem the Baker needs lots of bananas to bake an enormous loaf of banana bread. However, a delivery of bananas is stuck on a broken-down tractor(n)

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: A cowboy rides into Alphaland. Fun and games with the 26 letters that come to life, tell stories and make words(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘donkey' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure(n)

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: It's a lovely day for a picnic, but one of the flapjacks goes missing. Is there a flapjack-snaffler on the loose or has Three forgotten how to count?(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop teaches Bing and Pando how to blow bubbles, but frustration and outrage soon follow(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The bird-like characters see a hiker joining things onto his rucksack, and decide to add a few of their own. Narrated by Linda Bassett(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go on a bus trip to the shops, and Mr Tumble sets up his own outlet at home(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po do a roundy round dance and watch children in Tummy Tales ride a carousel which goes round and round too(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy has two pinecones for tapping, which turn out to be explorers. They lead the Book Animals on a journey to discover sounds and textures and Llama makes an aural map(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: Priya goes for her first ever haircut in preparation for starting nursery. She is a little nervous about what a haircut will feel like, so first she follows Daddy to the barbers to watch him first. Priya realises that, even though scissors are sharp, it doesn't hurt when they cut your hair. Now it doesn't seem so scary, so Priya and Dad talk about what kind of hairstyle she would like(n)

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: When the museum's prized prehistoric tumbleweed goes missing, Andy Day heads back 85 million years in time to try and find some more. During his search he helps a mother dinosaur protect her nest(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Sinker attempts to teach Cook and Line how to map read by setting them a course to navigate in the cabin. Meanwhile, the Swashbucklers are aiming to win back all Gem's jewels and earn the right to see one of the pirates walk the plank into the Ship's Mess(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is sick, so the squirrels make her a Get Well Soon card to cheer her up. However, on the way to visit her they meet a host of animals that also need help(n)

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Sailor Po can't sail her yacht unless Team Tinpo can find wind for her sails(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy and friends have to get Lofty's heart pumping so they can celebrate his birthday. Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster as he finds the answers to everything a pre-school child would want to know(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie Munnings shows off three amazing machines that all have sails(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The gang races to rescue Cotton-tail's blanket and escape from Mr Tod before a storm arrives(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch goes on a desert adventure to uncover a lost city, but is disappointed to learn someone else has found it already(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Live-action comedy sketch show for the under-sixes, starring Justin Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells take Waffle to school to ask the headteacher if Waffle can visit class(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Today it is Welsh Day, and the children in reception are celebrating the Welsh language(n)

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo imagines a world where people are all the same, but Felix and Lily help him to realise that being different is a good thing(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Each of the friends decide to give Lily Plant some pretend water'(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small Clanger finds a strange ball while out fishing in space. No one knows what it is so Major decides to put it on a shelf in his workshop. But a little while later he finds it in two hollow pieces on the ground - and the Soup Dragon discovers that something has been eating the copper trees. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy teaches Igglepiggle how to kick a ball around the garden(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Helen George reads a bedtime Story about a cat that has a special chair(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, featuring the women's 200m and men's 110m hurdles. Dina Asher-Smith entered the 200m as one of the favourites to prevail in a discipline where there had not been a British medallist since Kathy Smallwood-Cook took bronze in the inaugural 1983 event in Helsinki. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. The contestants are sent 6,000 miles from London to the capital city of Cape Town in South Africa, where they are tasked to set up and run their own safari and vineyard tours. One team's brash approach to ticket sales causes commotion, while on the other team premium prices prove problematic(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby and the In The Style team get to work on a collection for 21-year-old Lottie Tomlinson, a social media influencer with over three million followers. Hoping to impress her, Adam decides to send her on a trip to Ibiza to shoot the campaign. While Lottie is ultimately happy with the items, Adam is concerned the designs are very niche and may not prove as popular as their previous collection(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.desc: Zoe Ball dishes the backstage gossip, talks to some of the couples and has a sneak peek at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley present(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia throws a party for Bex's last day in Walford, where everyone is surprised by the return of Dot and her granddaughter Dotty. However, thing soon take a downward turn when the pressures of the past few months become too much for Bex to take. Sharon's plan to go to Portugal falls apart, so she begs Jack for help, Whitney is overjoyed to receive a text from Leo and Jean helps Daniel complete his bucket list(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present the consumer investigation show, exposing rogue traders and fighting for consumers' rights. Spencer Matthews explores how familiar stores are able to send targeted adverts to his phone, there are revelations about popular high-street names and families put another household essential through its paces(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: Documentary following officers from Britain's biggest and busiest police force as they deal with life, death, crime and its victims across London. Detectives from the Met's Sapphire team deal with the case of a distressed woman who has been raped by a stranger. Meanwhile, Croydon CID are tasked to find the suspect who robbed radio sports pundit Simon Jordan at gunpoint in broad daylight(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Wallasey, Wirral in Merseyside, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a property in Birmingham with some mesmerising wallpaper and a south Wales house that has her dancing with joy. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a place that has him a bit confused(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for a cocaine smuggler in the north-west of England leads to an arrest in a wealthy suburb of Accra in Ghana(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: A case in which a single mother was left terrified after her car broke down leaving her and her children stranded. The court has since said she should get her money back, but tempers flare when the enforcement officers visit the garage responsible. Plus, Billy and Michael have an early morning encounter with a particularly abusive debtor(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury fair, Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. Natasha also learns more about one of the UK's most-loved cartoonists(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara's acrimony takes an unexpected turn as Valerie reaches out to Jimmi. Ruhma comes across an expectant mother who is doing everything not to give birth despite being in labour(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property hunting in North Devon for a couple whose search for a country home on a £325,000 budget has so far fallen short. He also learns about a unique local pigment, known as Bideford Black, and enjoys the artistic freedom of painting on the beach(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Michelle Ackerley and Iain Stirling present(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams observes gulls rearing chicks and causing havoc on the streets of Aberystwyth, and on the outskirts of town, badgers are dodging cars on a busy main road as they set off on a nightly expedition for food in the town's housing estates. In Conwy, jackdaws have set up home on the castle walls. Pembroke Castle too has its jackdaws, and a beautiful mill pond that attracts otter families looking for eels(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Broadcaster Rylan Clark-Neal, comedian Sara Pascoe, retail consultant Mary Portas and comedian Paul Sinha join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The paramedics arrive at Dot's and as Bex is taken away in an ambulance, a stunned Louise watches on while Lisa is clearly racked with guilt. To make matters worse, Dotty mentions the pills found in Bex's room, leaving Louise shocked. Meanwhile, the Slaters remind Kush that Walford is no safe place for Martin, Sharon is desperate to meet Mel's demands and Jean is not prepared for what Daniel says to her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle in the last of the semi-finals, hoping to secure a place in finals week. The five remaining celebrities cook one dish of their own design for three of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Jay Rayner, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa - a daunting task for even the most adept chefs. Presenters John Torode and Gregg Wallace later determine which four will go through(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Karen and Geraldine are perplexed by who might have told the police about Mrs Bookham's missing money. However with Geraldine falsely accusing Lisa Marie, it's only a matter of time before they realise who the real culprit is. At the amusement arcade, the appearance of a last-minute candidate for a new staff vacancy causes some surprise. Meanwhile, Geraldine's financial worries look like they might be over when she meets with a mystery investor. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning Bridget Jones star Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael. Music is supplied by Elbow, who perform White Noise, White Heat(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas: HIV and Me
programme.desc: Documentary following former Wales rugby star Gareth Thomas as he opens up about living with HIV and sets about tackling the stigmas, myths and misunderstandings surrounding the virus. The programme follows Gareth as he prepares to take on the biggest physical challenge of his career - running the world's toughest Iron Man(n)

However, thing soon take a downward turn when the pressures of the past few months become too much for Bex to take. Sharon's plan to go to Portugal falls apart, so she begs Jack for help, Whitney is overjoyed to receive a text from Leo and Jean helps Daniel complete his bucket list(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present the consumer investigation show, exposing rogue traders and fighting for consumers' rights. Spencer Matthews explores how familiar stores are able to send targeted adverts to his phone, there are revelations about popular high-street names and families put another household essential through its paces(n)

programme.title: Tiny Lives
programme.desc: Documentary series offering unique access to the neonatal unit at University Hospital Wishaw. A baby born at 23 weeks fights for his life, a family with premature twins finally get to bring their son home after six months in the unit and rhesus baby Parker undergoes a rare procedure to have all of his contaminated blood exchanged(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Wallasey, Wirral in Merseyside, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a property in Birmingham with some mesmerising wallpaper and a south Wales house that has her dancing with joy. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a place that has him a bit confused(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for a cocaine smuggler in the north-west of England leads to an arrest in a wealthy suburb of Accra in Ghana(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: A case in which a single mother was left terrified after her car broke down leaving her and her children stranded. The court has since said she should get her money back, but tempers flare when the enforcement officers visit the garage responsible. Plus, Billy and Michael have an early morning encounter with a particularly abusive debtor(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury fair, Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. Natasha also learns more about one of the UK's most-loved cartoonists(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara's acrimony takes an unexpected turn as Valerie reaches out to Jimmi. Ruhma comes across an expectant mother who is doing everything not to give birth despite being in labour(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Seventeen contenders start this edition of the quiz, but how many of them will survive to face the £10,000 Question? Hosted by Rick Edwards(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property hunting in North Devon for a couple whose search for a country home on a £325,000 budget has so far fallen short. He also learns about a unique local pigment, known as Bideford Black, and enjoys the artistic freedom of painting on the beach(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Michelle Ackerley and Iain Stirling present(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: In the Borders region, Dougie Vipond heads to Langholm, home of the Scottish Chilli Trail, before joining Nick Nairn and the Landward Food Van in Moffat for a vegan chilli feast(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Broadcaster Rylan Clark-Neal, comedian Sara Pascoe, retail consultant Mary Portas and comedian Paul Sinha join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The paramedics arrive at Dot's and as Bex is taken away in an ambulance, a stunned Louise watches on while Lisa is clearly racked with guilt. To make matters worse, Dotty mentions the pills found in Bex's room, leaving Louise shocked. Meanwhile, the Slaters remind Kush that Walford is no safe place for Martin, Sharon is desperate to meet Mel's demands and Jean is not prepared for what Daniel says to her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Contenders battle in the last of the semi-finals, hoping to secure a place in finals week. The five remaining celebrities cook one dish of their own design for three of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Jay Rayner, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa - a daunting task for even the most adept chefs. Presenters John Torode and Gregg Wallace later determine which four will go through(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: Karen and Geraldine are perplexed by who might have told the police about Mrs Bookham's missing money. However with Geraldine falsely accusing Lisa Marie, it's only a matter of time before they realise who the real culprit is. At the amusement arcade, the appearance of a last-minute candidate for a new staff vacancy causes some surprise. Meanwhile, Geraldine's financial worries look like they might be over when she meets with a mystery investor. Comedy, starring Maggie Ollerenshaw, Catherine Tyldesley and Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning Bridget Jones star Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael. Music is supplied by Elbow, who perform White Noise, White Heat(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: The first episode of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge, based on her one-woman stage show about a woman wrestling with the demands of modern living through a mixture of bravado and casual sex. Fleabag struggles to make ends meet in the frenetic world of contemporary London as she tries to work out what she should be doing with her life. With Olivia Colman and Hugh Dennis(n)

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. The paniel is joined by social media's Robert Borthwick, a man known for his wit and whimsy on Twitter that riles and rallies fans of every club in equal measure(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming

programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined at an antiques fair at Wetherby Racecourse by experts Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt, who help two teams buy three items to take to auction in Darlington(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel notices that Becky is feeling smothered, and Valerie has a tough decision to make. Meanwhile, Emma spearheads a blood drive at The Campus(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple hunting for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £750,000, and visits a long-standing family-run bootmakers in the Peak District(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell head to an auction in Hamilton. Steph comes across a chic French sign, but Phil is distracted by the local cuisine(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The six remaining celebrity contestants take part in the semi-finals, and are challenged to recreate a dish by the world-renowned chef and food writer, Yotam Ottolenghi. For the second task, the celebrities place in the competition rests on a dish inspired by someone they love or admire(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of day six of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar, featuring the women's 200m and men's 110m hurdles. Dina Asher-Smith entered the 200m as one of the favourites to prevail in a discipline where there had not been a British medallist since Kathy Smallwood-Cook took bronze in the inaugural 1983 event in Helsinki. With analysis from Michael Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The business-based reality show returns. A new group of 16 hopeful candidates embark on their first challenge of the competition. programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby and the In The Style team get to work on a collection for 21-year-old Lottie Tomlinson, a social media influencer with over three million followers. Hoping to impress her, Adam decides to send her on a trip to Ibiza to shoot the campaign. While Lottie is ultimately happy with the items, Adam is concerned the designs are very niche and may not prove as popular as their previous collection(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the assassination of Jamal Khashoggi, Jane Corbin investigates the brutal murder of the journalist inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. She speaks to some of the very few people who have listened to covert recordings from inside the diplomatic building, revealing the full details of what happened to Khashoggi, the hit squad sent to kill him and the cover-up that followed. She also hears evidence that responsibility for the murder goes to the highest levels of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and asks whether the West has turned a blind eye to state-sanctioned murder so that lucrative trade deals can flourish(n)

programme.desc: Paul meets energetic five-year-old Cora, who has cerebral palsy. To try to improve her co-ordination and muscle control, Cora is having Botox injections in her leg as part of a pioneering research trial. The presenter also chats to 10-year-old George, who is at the hospital for the latest round of laser surgery to lessen his facial birthmarks, and hears about three-month-old Oliver, who was born with a cleft lip. Oliver is just one of 6,000 babies treated at Great Ormond Street every year, so Paul also meets the special team dedicated to meeting the feeding demands of all those hungry little mouths(n)

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: After finding an unusual tattoo on the college dean's body and two more murder victims with the same inking, Lewis and Hathaway discover that the tattoo signifies membership of a secret esoteric society that believes past sins can be forgiven by the literal sharing of guilt. Before the fourth and final murder takes place, the team must uncover the guilty secret that the victims share in order to learn the identity of the killer. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott head the cast of this action drama, now well into its third and final season. Murtaugh (Wayans) takes a family holiday, but is pulled back into work after witnessing an alleged abduction. Meanwhile, Cole (Scott) is left to investigate a murder by himself. Later, however, the two cases begin to intersect. Then, Erica's mother, Senator Malick comes to town and stirs things up between her daughter, Cole and the case at hand. Kevin Rahm, Johnathan Fernandez and Michelle Mitchenor also star(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Builder Rich from Birmingham, blacksmith Andy from Glastonbury, business manager Ashley from London and estate agent Ross from Huddersfield all vie for the attentions of 30 single women, intent on capturing their interest and securing a date. However, first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Host Paddy McGuinness monitors their progress and reviews the dating action from last week's couples on the Isle of Fernando's(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets energetic five-year-old Cora, who has cerebral palsy. To try to improve her co-ordination and muscle control, Cora is having Botox injections in her leg as part of a pioneering research trial. The presenter also chats to 10-year-old George, who is at the hospital for the latest round of laser surgery to lessen his facial birthmarks, and hears about three-month-old Oliver, who was born with a cleft lip. Oliver is just one of 6,000 babies treated at Great Ormond Street every year, so Paul also meets the special team dedicated to meeting the feeding demands of all those hungry little mouths(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: There's an alert at HMP Downview women's prison when repeat offender Collette O'Flaherty, distraught at missing out on a place at the prisoners' family day, takes a fellow inmate hostage. A prisoner at HMP Portland hears false rumours that he is a sex offender - and suspects the culprit spreading the lies is a new officer. The inmates at HMP Bullingdon look forward to Friday, when they receive small treats like chocolate and shower gel - but dealing in these items quickly leads to violence(n)

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on crashes, miracle escapes and bad driving, with video and interviews from victims and eyewitnesses. programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished final novel about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change - and becomes involved in the scheming of its ambitious inhabitants. Charlotte and Sidney find themselves at loggerheads but must work together in an attempt to rescue Miss Lambe. Tom tries to save Sanditon and his marriage, while Edward and Clara enter into a scandalous pact to finally get their hands on Lady Denham's inheritance(n)

Jim is concerned by his lack of sexual experience, and goes in search of an old flame, Oz struggles to keep a long-term relationship going, and Finch tries to master the art of tantric sex. Comedy sequel, starring Jason Biggs, Chris Klein, Seann William Scott, Alyson Hannigan and Eugene Levy(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Toby Jones and Phyllis Logan(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious road accident causes chaos for the residents of Aidensfield when a tanker containing a mysterious substance is breached, leaving a reservoir polluted and Ashfordly police tearing their hair out as they try to track down the culprit. Luckily, the staff of nearby St Aidan's Royal Free hospital are on hand to offer some much-needed assistance(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: James, Tim and Chris are exposed as having lied about their whereabouts on the night Hayley died. Pete reveals that he saw Eliot climbing into the holiday home through a window on New Year's Day, while leaves James struggling with the possibility that his son may have killed Hayley. Fran cracks a code in the dead girl's diary, suggesting that she met a local drug dealer on the night she disappeared(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: The team discovers a number of items that appear to belong to Hayley in Tim's house, providing the damning evidence needed to convict him of her murder. He tries to protest his innocence, claiming he bought them for his daughters at a fete. His story seems to be backed up when Jessica and her mother reveal that none of the items found belong to Hayley, leaving the investigation back at square one. Meanwhile, a new discovery has a shocking impact on the detectives, but proves especially devastating for Cassie(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of prison officer Charlie Tyner, Brooks and Devlin discover he had been abusing his position and convicts in his charge. A suspect is brought to trial, but Steel and Phillips go in pursuit of a career criminal connected to Tyner and have to persuade their key witness to give evidence against a man she claims to love. Starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress thinks Mike's replacement PC Steve Crane is too anxious to prove his worth as he investigates a spate of farm break-ins on the moors, and Vernon causes chaos trying to impress new arrival Dr Liz Merrick with his community spirit. Elsewhere, Mike grows disillusioned with CID work. James Carlton and Aislin McGuckin star as the newcomers(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack seems to have forgotten Sarah's birthday, Annie arrives at Emmerdale Farm and cannot understand why it has been abandoned, and Seth decides to move back home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is thrilled to hear Terry will be released on December 20th, Jenny fusses over pregnant Mitzi, and Andy tells Liz he is marrying Amy and is staying at Bettabuy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tanya is confident that Bet will keep her on, Liz accuses Curly of pushing Andy into ruining his life, and Colin has had enough of Liz going on about Andy and Amy(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress thinks Mike's replacement PC Steve Crane is too anxious to prove his worth as he investigates a spate of farm break-ins on the moors, and Vernon causes chaos trying to impress new arrival Dr Liz Merrick with his community spirit. Elsewhere, Mike grows disillusioned with CID work. James Carlton and Aislin McGuckin star as the newcomers(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey organises an exhibition as the bus company celebrates its half-centenary. Stan reminisces, and on falling asleep finds himself at the wheel of a bus from the turn of the 20th century. Comedy. starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: New tenant Ambrose bamboozles Rigsby by claiming to be a mystic, and soon has the other lodgers under a strange spell. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Dervish and Co are back at the crease but with ball tampering and rule bending rife, a fair game seems out of the question. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger worries about who will make the team's teas when Mim is taken ill. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah argue about what they should tell Annie about the farmhouse. Eric's attempts at reconciliation with Elizabeth are not working(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Archie is upset at the way Zoe has treated him, Frank is furious with Joe, Chris warns Joe to stay away from Kim, and Annie feels bitter towards her family(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken offers Deirdre financial help but she refuses it, Jack treats Vera to a day in Blackpool, and Rita is annoyed by the way Jenny takes advantage of Mavis(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey believes Alf cannot live without the shop, while Liz and Jim call a family conference, and then spend the evening together(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The villagers of Aspern Tallow begin to believe the ghost of a former resident has come back to haunt them. Barnaby and Troy have to determine why packing cases are apparently combusting spontaneously - and whether it is more than a coincidence that the battered body of a relative of the dead man has been found in the family vault. With John Nettles, Prunella Scales and Patricia Brake(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for harvest time, but Steph struggles to cope with the workload and faces losing the farm if she cannot meet her quota. A newcomer to the community stirs up tension between Sarah and Frances, while Joyce and the Brindsleys also receive unexpected visitors. Teresa faces a big decision, while Sarah finds herself in a dangerous situation. Alison discovers the real reason behind the parachute accidents and makes a horrifying connection to the factory. She races to warn Frances before it is too late(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The village prepares for Nick and Teresa's wedding as the Battle of Britain draws to an end. Pat faces a big decision as Marek prepares to leave, but Bob intends to ruin her plans. Frances fights to clear her name following the closure of the factory, Alison wrestles with her conscience, and a chance encounter leaves Joyce with some unanswered questions(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.desc: Ventress thinks Mike's replacement PC Steve Crane is too anxious to prove his worth as he investigates a spate of farm break-ins on the moors, and Vernon causes chaos trying to impress new arrival Dr Liz Merrick with his community spirit. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for harvest time, but Steph struggles to cope with the workload and faces losing the farm if she cannot meet her quota. A newcomer to the community stirs up tension between Sarah and Frances, while Joyce and the Brindsleys also receive unexpected visitors. Teresa faces a big decision, while Sarah finds herself in a dangerous situation. Alison discovers the real reason behind the parachute accidents and makes a horrifying connection to the factory. She races to warn Frances before it is too late(n)

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The village prepares for Nick and Teresa's wedding as the Battle of Britain draws to an end. Pat faces a big decision as Marek prepares to leave, but Bob intends to ruin her plans. Frances fights to clear her name following the closure of the factory, Alison wrestles with her conscience, and a chance encounter leaves Joyce with some unanswered questions(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney arrive in Texas, where they enjoy driving around in tanks, experience space travel in a centrifuge and hear campfire tales about UFOs. First up, the pair undertake some centrifuge training, which is used to prepare astronauts for the crushing gravitational forces produced at take-off from the extreme exhilaration. They also head to the outskirts of Houston to meet some UFO enthusiasts who investigate claimed sightings of extra-terrestrials, and are gripped by stories of spaceships blocking roads and people being taken by aliens for experiments(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney embark on the final leg of their journey, which sees the pair arrive in the Louisiana city of New Orleans. Here, they visit a spiritual voodoo priest for a spot of energy cleansing, and feed alligators in the Bayou with Captain Billy, who knows every corner of the wetlands, having been born and raised on the bayous of South Louisiana. Later, Bradley hops on board the Natchez jazz Steamboat, before the pair take to the streets to lead a spectacular parade with a six-piece band(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney embark on the final leg of their journey, which sees the pair arrive in the Louisiana city of New Orleans. Here, they visit a spiritual voodoo priest for a spot of energy cleansing, and feed alligators in the Bayou with Captain Billy, who knows every corner of the wetlands, having been born and raised on the bayous of South Louisiana. programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents the general knowledge quiz as famous faces from children's television programmes take part. This edition features CBBC presenters Anthea Turner and Andy Crane, CBeebies stars Justin Fletcher and Gemma Hunt, children's TV hosts Dave Benson-Phillips and Gareth Jones, and former presenters Sally James and Geoffrey Hayes. The famous faces try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Co-host Richard Osman has all the answers(n)
